properties.name: Bruce Nuclear Generating Station
properties.description: Largest nuclear plant in the world (by output). 8 CANDU reactors at Tiverton, Lake Huron.

properties.name: Pickering Nuclear Generating Station
properties.description: 6 operating CANDU units on Lake Ontario, near Toronto.

properties.name: Darlington Nuclear Generating Station
properties.description: 4 CANDU units east of Toronto. Site of planned small modular reactors.

properties.name: Chalk River Laboratories
properties.description: Atomic Energy of Canada Ltd. Major research facility on the Ottawa River. NRU reactor (retired 2018), now home to new SMR design centre.

properties.name: Point Lepreau Nuclear Generating Station
properties.description: Only nuclear plant in Atlantic Canada. 1 CANDU-6 unit on the Bay of Fundy.

properties.name: Gentilly-2 Nuclear Generating Station
properties.description: CANDU reactor near Trois-Rivières. Shut down in 2012, now in decommissioning phase.

properties.name: Whiteshell Laboratories
properties.description: AECL research facility near Pinawa, Manitoba. WR-1 reactor shut down 1985.

properties.name: McArthur River Uranium Mine
properties.description: World's largest high-grade uranium mine, northern Saskatchewan.

properties.name: Cigar Lake Uranium Mine
properties.description: High-grade underground uranium mine, northern Saskatchewan.

properties.name: Key Lake Uranium Mill
properties.description: Uranium milling operation, processes ore from McArthur River.

properties.name: Rabbit Lake Uranium Mine
properties.description: First uranium mine in Saskatchewan's Athabasca Basin (1975).

properties.name: Port Hope Area C / Welcome Waste Facility
properties.description: Historic radium and uranium processing site. Long-term low-level radioactive waste management.

properties.name: Darlington SMR Demonstration
properties.description: Canada's first Small Modular Reactor demonstration project, planned at Darlington site.
